content:
Hey, I need to use a cryptographic hash function (I have no requirement for a specific one, probably SHA-512/256 if that is reasonably simple to use, otherwise probably just SHA-256), and was looking for a way to do that using existing kernel facilities.

I found this patch series by @FUJITA Tomonori, which seems to have never been merged, but also seems to be the most recent version. Is there something more recent, or is that what I should be working with?
(Not sure if this fits General or Help better, feel free to move this)
That patch is the latest version. Since there are no users of the Rust hash API, the patch has not been merged.
Makes sense, then I'll use that, thank you.
Does the crypto API allow calculating SHA-512/256? I saw that that is usually faster on 64bit systems than SHA-256, but it seems like that's not available.
I think that it does but not sure.
